name: Justin Wilson's Hush Puppies
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all dry ingredients in a large bowl.
2. In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs, buttermilk, chopped green onion, bacon drippings, and garlic powder.
3. Gradually add the wet mixture to the dry mixture, stirring until just combined.
4. Heat the deep fat for frying according to your fryer's instructions.
5. Drop spoonfuls of batter into the hot oil.
6. Fry until golden brown on all sides.
Note: This recipe makes approximately 48 hush puppies. The term "hush puppy" originated in the Southern United States after the Civil War when people had little food for themselves or their dogs, and they would give pieces of fried cornbread to quiet hungry dogs.
